description: Automated response to Aurora Global DB replication lag or connection exhaustion alarms. Verifies replica health, triggers DR failover Lambda if needed, and notifies stakeholders.

steps.name: notify_oncall_immediate
steps.description: Immediately alert on-call engineer and open PagerDuty P1 incident.

params.message: CRITICAL: Aurora database alarm triggered. Automated runbook starting. Verify database health and consider failover.

steps.name: notify_slack
steps.description: Post alert to Slack incident channel with context.

params.message: Aurora alarm detected. Runbook executing. Check AWS Console → RDS → Clusters for current status. If replication lag > 5s and primary is unavailable, execute failover Lambda.

steps.name: invoke_health_check
steps.description: Invoke the health check aggregator Lambda to assess current DB connectivity.

manual_escalation_steps: If CPU > 95% for > 5 minutes: invoke securebase-prod-failover-orchestrator Lambda
If replication lag > 30s: execute failover (see DR_RUNBOOK.md)
After failover: verify replica promotion via RDS console
Update Route 53 / API connection strings if not handled automatically
Notify customer success team if customer-facing impact > 5 minutes
failover_command: aws lambda invoke --function-name securebase-prod-failover-orchestrator --payload '{"action": "failover", "reason": "Aurora runbook auto-trigger"}' --cli-binary-format raw-in-base64-out /tmp/failover-result.json
